name: Hannibal

documentation.readme: Hannibal is a x64 Windows Agent written in fully position independent C (plus a tiny bit of C++). It is based off the Stardust template created by @C5pider.
usage_sections: Installation: To install Hannibal, you'll need Mythic installed on a remote computer. You can find installation instructions for Mythic at the Mythic project page. From the Mythic install directory, use the following command to install Hannibal as the root user: From the Mythic install directo

arguments.name: -Issues
arguments.description: Build Times - Build times can be slow (~2min @ 2core 2gb) depending on the hardware you're compiling on. This is due to it being tricky to handle incremental compilation due to add

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./mythic-cli install github $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Further invocations from upstream: `sudo -E ./mythic-cli install github $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: ![Hannibal](documentation-payload/hannibal/Hannibal.svg).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Hannibal is a x64 Windows Agent written in fully position independent C (plus a tiny bit of C++). It is based off the Stardust template created by @C5pider.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use Hannibal because ![Hannibal](documentation-payload/hannibal/Hannibal.svg).
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./mythic-cli install github $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for Hannibal.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo -E ./mythic-cli install github $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for Hannibal.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair Hannibal with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Hannibal --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
